elements.section-2411-002: General discussion on CM abelian varieties and their periods
elements.sent-2411-084: In this section, we recall some standard facts and results on CM pairs and CM abelian varieties.
elements.sent-2411-085: Let $A$ be a CM abelian variety of dimension $g$.
elements.sent-2411-086: A number field $E$ is called a CM field if it is an imaginary quadratic extension of a totally real number field.
elements.sent-2411-087: A {\em CM algebra is a finite product of CM fields.}
elements.sent-2411-088: A {\em CM type on a CM algebra $E$ is a subset $\Phi =\{\phi_1,\dots,\phi_g\} \subseteq \Hom(E,\CC)$ such that $\Hom(E,\CC)=\Phi \sqcup \overline{\Phi}$ (where $\overline{\Phi = \{\bar{\phi _1,\ldots,\bar{\phi _g\}$ is the complex conjugate of $\Phi$).}}}}
elements.sent-2411-089: A {\em CM pair is a pair $(E,\Phi)$ consisting of a CM algebra $E$ and a CM type $\Phi$.}
elements.sent-2411-090: Two CM pairs $(E,\Phi)$ and $(E',\Phi')$ are said to be isomorphic if there exists an isomorphism $\alpha \colon E \rightarrow E'$ of $\QQ$-algebras such that $\phi \circ \alpha \in \Phi$ whenever $\phi \in \Phi'$.
elements.sent-2411-091: To each CM pair $(E,\Phi)$, we can associate a CM abelian variety $A_{(E,\Phi) := \CC^{[E:\QQ]/2 /\Phi(\cO_E)$ with $\cO_E$ the ring of integers of $E$.}}
elements.sent-2411-092: A CM pair $(E,\Phi)$ is said to be {\em primitive if there does not exist a proper sub-CM algebra $E_0$ of $E$ such that $\Phi|_{E_0 := \{\phi|_{E_0 : \phi \in \Phi\}$ is a CM type on $E_0$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-093: A CM pair $(E,\Phi)$ is primitive if and only if $A_{(E,\Phi)}$ is simple.
elements.sent-2411-094: Finally, the {\em Galois closure $E^c$ of a CM algebra $E$ is defined as follows: $E = E_1^{n_1}\times \cdots \times E_m^{n_m}$ with each $E_j$ a CM field, and $E^c$ is defined to be the composite of the Galois closures of $E_1,\ldots,E_m$.}
